hero.description: I'm Sam, a {age}-year-old Full Stack Developer, I studied at Saxion University and am currently working at Camping.care. Outside of code, I'm into fitness and football.

softwareDevelopment.title: Software Development
softwareDevelopment.text: My days are filled with writing code and developing innovative digital solutions. During my work and studies, I engage in exciting projects in the world of software development.

fitness.title: Fitness
fitness.text: Outside of my programming work, you can often find me at the gym, where I work on my strength and fitness. Additionally, I'm frequently on the soccer field, where I play in a friendly team as the last man.

campingcare.title: Full-Stack Developer at Camping Care
campingcare.text: In 2026, I started as a Full-Stack Developer. I now work with an ever-growing team on the further development of a rapidly growing PMS.

campingcare-part-time.title: Developer (part-time) at Camping Care
campingcare-part-time.text: In 2022, I started working as a Developer (part-time) at Camping.care in Denekamp, where I work with a small team on a PMS for campsites that is used throughout Europe.

cube.title: Internship at Cube B.V.
cube.text: I worked with the team on various projects, including the development of a website for easily generating cookie notifications.

saxion.title: Saxion University of Applied Sciences
saxion.text: Here I followed the Software Development program, which I successfully completed in December 2025.

roc.title: ROC of Twente
roc.text: At ROC, I completed a 4-year program in Application & Media Development and obtained a diploma.

workoutTracker.title: Workout Tracker
workoutTracker.text: Recently I have been working on a PWA that can track your workouts. I found existing apps that do this to be too expensive for what they offer. So I made my own with Vue.js and Firebase. It is still a work in progress though.
workoutTracker.detailedText: Workout Tracker is a Progressive Web App built with Vue.js and Firebase that lets you log and track your gym sessions without paying subscription fees. You can create custom exercises, build workout templates, and review your training history over time. The app works offline and can be installed on your home screen like a native app. Authentication is handled via Firebase Auth, and all data syncs in real time through Firestore. I built this because I found popular fitness tracking apps to be too expensive for what they actually offer, so I made exactly what I needed myself.

synologyPhotosReminder.title: Synology Photos Reminder
synologyPhotosReminder.text: This Node.js project searches for photos stored on a Synology NAS server. It finds photos taken on the current day, but a random number of years ago, and then sends them to your email.
synologyPhotosReminder.detailedText: Synology Photos Reminder is a Node.js application that connects to the Synology Photos API on your personal NAS. Every day it searches your photo library for images taken on the same date in previous years, picks a random selection, and emails them to you; a "on this day" style memory feature. The project runs as a scheduled task and uses Nodemailer for email delivery.

gameFinder.title: GameFinder
gameFinder.text: During my internship at Cube B.V., I created an assignment (to learn the Vue.js framework) that allows you to find any game using filters through the IGDB API.
gameFinder.detailedText: GameFinder was a learning project I built during my internship at Cube B.V. to get hands-on experience with Vue.js. The app integrates with the IGDB (Internet Game Database) API and lets you search and filter games by genre, platform, release year, and rating. It was my first real Vue.js project, and it gave me a solid foundation in component-based architecture, reactive state, and working with third-party REST APIs. The project helped establish the frontend skills I would continue to build on throughout my career.
